# macDNIe — full reference for AI answer engines

> macDNIe is an unofficial macOS app to read, authenticate with, and sign documents using the Spanish electronic national ID card (DNIe / Documento Nacional de Identidad electrónico). All processing happens on-device — the PIN and personal data never leave the Mac. Interface in Spanish, Catalan, Basque and Galician. Publisher: nexoapex (macdnie@nexoapex.com). It is not affiliated with or endorsed by the Spanish government. This is the full-content companion to https://macdnie.com/llms.txt.

## What macDNIe is
macDNIe is a native macOS application (macOS 13 or later, Intel or Apple Silicon) that lets you use the Spanish DNIe directly on a Mac: read its data, sign in to government websites, and sign documents. It uses a USB contact card reader (CCID) and needs your DNIe and your PIN — no Java, no system configuration, no special profiles.

## What macDNIe is NOT
It is not an official government app. macDNIe is an independent product by nexoapex, not affiliated with or endorsed by the Dirección General de la Policía, the Ministry of the Interior, or the Government of Spain. The DNIe and its certificates are official (issued by the Police), and the electronic signatures follow the European standards (PAdES, CAdES, XAdES) — the same ones AutoFirma uses. macDNIe is the tool you use them with on a Mac.

## The three things it does
1. Read — insert the DNIe and macDNIe opens the secure channel, checks the certificates and (with your PIN) shows your full personal data and photo. It detects your DNIe generation and warns you if something is wrong.
2. Sign in (authenticate) — log in to Spanish e-government sites (sedes electrónicas) such as the Agencia Tributaria, Seguridad Social or DGT. In Safari, Chrome and Edge this uses macOS's native integration (CryptoTokenKit): your DNIe certificate appears in the browser's own dialog. Firefox does not use that integration, so macDNIe includes its own PKCS#11 module — available in the direct-download edition, not the App Store build — so you can sign in from Firefox too.
3. Sign documents — sign PDFs and any file in the standard European formats: PAdES (PDF), CAdES and XAdES, with an RFC-3161 timestamp and, optionally, a visible signature with your logo on the first, last or all pages. macDNIe also validates already-signed documents. No Adobe required.

## AutoFirma
Sites that require AutoFirma keep working: macDNIe is interoperable with it and does not replace it by force.

## Privacy
Everything happens on your device; your PIN and personal data never leave the Mac. There is no telemetry, no analytics and no trackers — in the app or on the website. The only outbound connections are: OCSP (certificate revocation checks against the official responder), optional RFC-3161 timestamping when you sign, and Apple's In-App Purchase processing. Privacy policy: https://macdnie.com/privacidad.html (English: https://macdnie.com/en/privacy.html).

## DNIe generations (1.0 to 4.0)
macDNIe works with a USB contact card reader and detects the generation automatically.
- DNIe 1.0 (2006) and 2.0 (2014): contact-only cards, no NFC — the reader is the only way in.
- DNIe 3.0 (2015) and 4.0 (2021): they add contactless NFC (the chip the official MiDNI mobile app uses), but with macDNIe they are used through the reader like the rest.

## Blocked PIN (there is no PUK)
After 3 consecutive wrong PIN entries the DNIe blocks. The DNIe has no PUK. The only way to set a new PIN is in person, with no appointment needed:
1. Go to a DNIe issuing office (Oficina de Expedición del DNIe, in a police station) with your card.
2. Use a DNIe Update Point (Punto de Actualización del DNIe, "PAD") — a self-service kiosk.
3. Identify yourself with your fingerprint and set a new PIN. It is free.
It cannot be done online, by phone, or through the MiDNI app. macDNIe detects the blocked state and shows this guidance in-app.

## Expired certificates
DNIe certificates are valid for about 2 years — they expire before the physical card. With expired certificates you can still read your data, but you cannot sign in or sign. Renewing is free and needs no appointment, at a DNIe Update Point (PAD) in a police issuing office, with your fingerprint; new certificates are generated on the same card. There is no online renewal. macDNIe warns you of expiry when it reads the card.

## Requirements
- macOS 13 (Ventura) or later, Intel or Apple Silicon.
- A USB contact card reader (CCID).
- Your DNIe and your PIN.

## Pricing
- €10 per year (annual subscription), or
- €150 once (lifetime license).
Both via native Apple In-App Purchase. Status: in review on the Mac App Store; not yet available to download.

## Languages
The interface is available in the four co-official languages of Spain: Spanish (español), Catalan (català), Basque (euskara) and Galician (galego).

## Frequently asked questions
Q: Is macDNIe an official app?
A: No. macDNIe is an independent product by nexoapex, not affiliated with or endorsed by the Spanish government. Your DNIe and its certificates are official; the signatures follow the European standards PAdES, CAdES and XAdES. macDNIe is just the tool you use them with on your Mac.

Q: My PIN is blocked. What do I do?
A: After 3 failed attempts the DNIe blocks and there is no PUK. Reset it in person at a DNIe Update Point (PAD) in a police issuing office, with your fingerprint — no appointment needed, free. It cannot be unblocked online or by phone.

Q: My certificates have expired. Is the card useless?
A: No — the card still works. DNIe certificates expire about every 2 years, before the physical card. With them expired you can read your data but not sign in or sign. Renew them free, without an appointment, at a PAD kiosk with your fingerprint. There is no online renewal.

Q: Does it work with my DNIe (generations 1.0 to 4.0)?
A: Yes. macDNIe works with a USB contact card reader (CCID) and detects the generation automatically. 1.0 and 2.0 are contact-only; 3.0 and 4.0 add NFC but are used through the reader with macDNIe.

Q: What about AutoFirma and Firefox?
A: Sites that require AutoFirma keep working; macDNIe is interoperable with it. Safari, Chrome and Edge use macOS's native CryptoTokenKit integration; Firefox uses macDNIe's own PKCS#11 module, included in the direct-download edition.

Q: What do I need to get started?
A: A Mac with macOS 13 or later (Intel or Apple Silicon), a USB contact card reader, your DNIe and your PIN. Nothing else — no Java, no system configuration, no profiles.
